budget_wines.name: CVNE Cune Crianza
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from CVNE in Haro's Barrio de la Estacion and through wide distribution across Spain, the UK and the United States.
budget_wines.description: The benchmark everyday Rioja: a year in oak gives gentle vanilla over bright Tempranillo cherry. Endlessly available and reliably under twelve euros, it is the bottle that taught the world what classic Crianza tastes like.
budget_wines.tip: If you can find the Vina Real Crianza from the same house, it is a slightly plusher sibling at a similar price.

budget_wines.name: Bodegas Muga Reserva
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct at Bodegas Muga in Haro's station district and through specialist merchants worldwide.
budget_wines.description: Made the old way with oak fermentation, egg-white fining and barrel ageing, Muga Reserva delivers genuine Haro-house pedigree for around twenty euros. Silky red fruit, cedar and a fine, savoury length well above its price.
budget_wines.tip: The Reserva is the value pick; the same cellar's pricier Prado Enea and Torre Muga are for special occasions.

budget_wines.name: La Rioja Alta Vina Alberdi Reserva
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from La Rioja Alta in Haro and through international fine-wine merchants.
budget_wines.description: A Reserva sold at a Crianza price: two years in American oak from a century-old Haro house, all sweet spice, dried cherry and polish. Repeatedly cited among the best-value Riojas in international press.
budget_wines.tip: Drink it now or hold five years; Vina Alberdi ages more gracefully than most wines near twenty euros.

budget_wines.name: Bodegas LAN Crianza
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Bodegas LAN at Fuenmayor and through broad Spanish and export distribution.
budget_wines.description: A dependable, modern-leaning Crianza from Fuenmayor: ripe dark cherry, smooth oak and an easy finish that overdelivers under twelve euros. A staple weeknight Rioja.
budget_wines.tip: LAN's a Mano and Culmen sit above this in the range; for value the standard Crianza is the one to stock by the case.

budget_wines.name: El Coto Crianza
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from El Coto de Rioja at Oyon and through large-scale distribution across Spain and abroad.
budget_wines.description: One of Spain's best-selling Riojas for good reason: pure Tempranillo, soft oak, juicy red fruit and a clean finish that makes it a faultless under-ten-euro pour for a crowd.
budget_wines.tip: The single-varietal Tempranillo style is friendlier and fruitier than blended Crianzas; a safe bet for newcomers to Rioja.

budget_wines.name: CVNE Monopole Blanco
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from CVNE in Haro and through wide international distribution.
budget_wines.description: Spain's oldest white-wine brand, Monopole is a crisp, lightly textured Viura with citrus and a saline snap. A reminder that white Rioja is one of the country's great value categories.
budget_wines.tip: Serve it cold with anchovies or grilled white fish; at this price it is a brilliant aperitif white.

budget_wines.name: Marques de Riscal Reserva
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Marques de Riscal at Elciego and through extensive global distribution.
budget_wines.description: From the 1858-founded Elciego house, a classic Reserva of dried cherry, leather and sweet oak that still slips under the price of many lesser wines. The signature gold-wire mesh is a centuries-old anti-counterfeit mark.
budget_wines.tip: The wire netting around the bottle is heritage, not gimmick; it dates to the estate's nineteenth-century origins.

budget_wines.name: Marques de Murrieta Reserva
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Marques de Murrieta at Ygay near Logrono and through fine-wine merchants worldwide.
budget_wines.description: From the estate descended from Rioja's first commercial bodega, a poised, traditional Reserva of red fruit, spice and fine tannin that punches well above its sub-twenty-five-euro price.
budget_wines.tip: This is the gateway to the house; the flagship Castillo Ygay Gran Reserva is a serious step up in price and ageing.

budget_wines.name: Bodegas Lacus Inedito
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Bodegas Lacus at Aldeanueva de Ebro in Rioja Oriental and through natural-wine importers.
budget_wines.description: An organically farmed Rioja Oriental Garnacha from a tiny family cellar, fresh and unadorned in the new-wave style. Proof that low-intervention Rioja can be had well under twenty euros.
budget_wines.tip: Lacus is a Rioja Oriental name worth seeking out for honest, terroir-driven wines at fair prices.

budget_wines.name: Bodegas LAN Gran Reserva
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Bodegas LAN at Fuenmayor and through Spanish and export distribution.
budget_wines.description: A genuine Gran Reserva, with the long oak and bottle ageing that name demands, for under twenty-five euros. Mature dried fruit, tobacco and leather make it one of Rioja's great value cellar-aged bottles.
budget_wines.tip: Gran Reserva under twenty-five euros is rare; this is an easy way to taste the most aged tier of Rioja without splurging.
